name: Tequila

palette.name: Blue Agave
palette.rationale: Blue-gray agave fields are the dominant landscape reference around Tequila.

palette.name: Volcanic Earth
palette.rationale: Warm reddish-brown soil and volcanic context ground the agave landscape.

palette.name: Añejo Gold
palette.rationale: Golden spirit and barrel-aging associations provide a clear product-culture accent.

palette.name: Pueblo Stucco
palette.rationale: Light stucco walls help translate the town center into a flexible neutral.

palette.name: Sierra Green
palette.rationale: A deeper green references the surrounding slopes and cultivated valley edges.

palette.name: Barrel Oak
palette.rationale: Oak brown adds a restrained distillery and barrel-room layer for product-adjacent design.

sources.title: Tequila, Jalisco
sources.publisher: Secretaría de Turismo, Gobierno de México
sources.note: Used for Pueblo Mágico and tourism context.

sources.title: Agave Landscape and Ancient Industrial Facilities of Tequila
sources.note: Used for agave landscape and industrial heritage context.

notes: Standard starter record; colors are editorial interpretations of landscape, architecture, and tequila culture, not official municipal colors.
editorialSummary: Tequila's standard palette turns agave blue, volcanic earth, añejo gold, pueblo stucco, sierra green, and barrel oak into a practical heritage-and-product color system.
